nodes.name: GitLab MR Review Webhook
nodes.notes: Production URL: https://k14b105.p.ssafy.io/n8n/webhook/gitlab-mr-review. Configure GitLab Webhook Secret validation with a credential or environment-backed gate before enabling production use.

nodes.name: Build Event Gate
nodes.notes: Do not commit a real GitLab Webhook Secret here. Replace the placeholder after import using n8n credential/env-backed configuration.

nodes.name: Get MR Changes
nodes.notes: After import, select the real n8n GitLab API credential. Do not store GitLab PAT in this workflow JSON.

parameters.jsCode:
const files = $json.files || [];
const skipped = $json.skipped_files || [];
const compactDiffs = files.map((file) => [
  `File: ${file.file_path}`,
  `Status: ${file.change_type || 'modified'}`,
  file.is_summarized ? 'Note: diff was summarized.' : '',
  'Diff:',
  file.diff || '',
].filter(Boolean).join('\n')).join('\n\n---\n\n');

const prompt = `
You are an experienced Korean senior engineer leaving a GitLab MR review comment.

Your goal is not to produce a checklist.
Your goal is to sound like a thoughtful teammate who actually read the diff and is trying to help the author make the change safer.

Write in Korean.
Do not use a report title.
Do not use markdown headings like "## 전체 의견".
Start directly with a natural sentence, optionally with one light emoji.

Review style:
- First, infer the author's intent from the diff and mention it briefly.
- Acknowledge the direction if it is reasonable, but do not overpraise.
- Then discuss the most important risks or questions.
- Prefer 2-4 thoughtful paragraphs or bullets, not a rigid template.
- Use concrete code/file references when useful.
- Use collaborative wording.
- Avoid sounding like a scanner.
- Do not mention skipped files unless it affects review confidence.
- If no meaningful issues exist, still give a useful review.
- You may use at most one light emoji in the whole comment, preferably in the first or last sentence.

What to look for:
- correctness bugs
- missing validation or edge cases
- transaction/session/token consistency
- auth/security regressions
- API response contract changes
- runtime/deployment risks
- tests that should exist because the diff changed behavior

SSAfer context:
- Backend: Spring Boot / Java 21
- AI server: FastAPI worker
- Frontend: React/Vite
- Infra: Docker Compose, NGINX, Jenkins, n8n, EC2 split deployment
- Public traffic enters through NGINX 443
- Jenkins runs under /jenkins/
- n8n runs under /n8n/
- n8n /n8n/ proxy must strip prefix to upstream /rest/...
- Spring 8080 and FastAPI 8000 must be restricted by private network/UFW/SG
- .env.example must never contain real secrets

Important constraints:
- Only make comments supported by the provided diff.
- If intent is unclear, ask a specific question instead of assuming a bug.
- Do not repeat the MR description.
- Do not invent line numbers.
- Keep the final comment concise enough for a GitLab MR discussion, but not shallow.

MR:
- Title: ${$json.title}
- Description: ${$json.description || '(none)'}
- Source: ${$json.source_branch}
- Target: ${$json.target_branch}
- Changed files: ${$json.changed_file_count}
- Reviewed files: ${$json.reviewed_file_count}
- Skipped files: ${skipped.map(file => `${file.path} (${file.reason})`).join(', ') || '(none)'}

Diffs:
${compactDiffs || '(No reviewable diff after filtering.)'}
`;

return [{ json: { ...$json, review_prompt: prompt } }];

nodes.name: Call Review LLM
nodes.notes: After import, select the real Header Auth credential. The credential must set Authorization: Bearer <GMS_KEY>.
